page: Amazon Web Services m6idn.xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 4 vCPUs across 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz and includes a 54 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, translating to 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, it includes a bundled 237 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps with EBS optimization.

Performance benchmarks indicate a divergence between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is moderate, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1892.08 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1601.0, both falling into the average tier. However, multi-core scaling is limited, as evidenced by a weak multi-core stress-ng score of 3862.58 and a weak Geekbench multi-core score of 3388.0. Memory bandwidth tests show a weak read-only large block transfer rate of 30.03 GB/sec, though large block write bandwidth remains average at 25.36 GB/sec. The instance demonstrates average performance in single-threaded compression and decompression tasks, but multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is poor.

Given the hardware profile and benchmark results, the m6idn.xlarge is suited for general-purpose workloads that benefit from local NVMe storage and moderate single-threaded CPU performance, such as static web serving, where it achieves an average throughput of 6.34 GB/sec. It also supports light machine learning tasks, processing 379.86 tokens per second for prompt processing on a small 135M LLM model. However, due to weak multi-core scaling and lower database operation scores, it is less optimal for heavy multi-threaded compute, intensive database operations, or large-scale compilation tasks.
description: The Amazon Web Services m6idn.xlarge is a general-purpose instance built on the Nitro hypervisor, featuring a 4-vCPU Intel Xeon 8375C processor with a 3.5 GHz clock speed and 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory. It includes a bundled 237 GB NVMe SSD instance store and offers 6 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks reveal average single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1601.0, but weaker multi-core scaling, as shown by a multi-core score of 3388.0. Memory write bandwidth is average at 25.36 GB/sec, while read bandwidth is weaker. The instance is suited for general-purpose applications, static web serving, and lightweight LLM inference, though its weak multi-core performance makes it less suitable for highly parallelized compute or intensive database workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6idn.xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring a 4-vCPU Intel Xeon processor, 16 GB DDR4 RAM, a 237 GB NVMe SSD instance store, and 6 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6idn.xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 4 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and a 237 GB NVMe SSD, optimized for network and EBS workloads.
tagline: A general-purpose instance combining dedicated Intel Xeon processing with local NVMe SSD storage and optimized network performance for balanced workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 4 vCPUs and 3.5 GHz clock speed
16.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 237 GB local NVMe SSD storage
6 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with EBS optimization
Average single-core CPU and text processing performance
Best suited for static web serving and lightweight general-purpose applications
Weaker multi-core scaling limits suitability for highly parallel workloads
